response.justification: The candidate is a versatile senior full-stack developer with a strong testing culture and some genuine high-throughput systems experience, but he does not meet the core minimum qualifications for this role. His professional C++ exposure amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments plus a thesis project, versus the required 8 years, and his recent work (Stripe, Shopify, RIDE) is TypeScript/Node product engineering rather than cloud compute platform or infrastructure work. He also lacks a formal technical-leadership track record leading platform teams, making him a poor fit despite solid general engineering craft.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career spanning web, embedded, desktop, and backend systems
Real low-latency, high-throughput experience (micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong testing and mentoring record: TDD practice, open-source test frameworks, workshops and courses
concerns: Falls far short of the required 8 years of C++; professional C/C++ experience is under one year
Recent experience is product-focused full-stack work, not large-scale cloud compute or infrastructure architecture
No formal technical-leadership role owning platform strategy, priorities, and cross-team alignment

key_factors.factor: Limited cloud platform and infrastructure leadership
Testing, mentoring, and high-throughput systems craft
